title: galium trachyspermum var. hispidum
intro: Galium trachyspermum var. hispidum, also known as Rakta Chandana, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, commonly found in the Himalayan region and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Galium trachyspermum var. hispidum include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, skin disorders, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be used to make medicines, tonics, and other remedies for various health conditions.
